If the response does not have status 200, does not have a media type of `application/json` or, if the body cannot be parsed as a JSON object, then discovery fails and Terraform considers the host to not support _any_ Terraform-native services.

If the response is an HTTP redirect then Terraform repeats this step with the new location as its discovery URL. Terraform is guaranteed to follow at least one redirect, but nested redirects are not guaranteed nor recommended.

If the response is a valid JSON object then its keys are Terraform native service identifiers, consisting of a service type name and a version string separated by a period. For example, the service identifier for version 1 of the module registry protocol is `modules.v1`.

The value of each object element is the base URL for the service in question. This URL may be either absolute or relative, and if relative it is resolved against the final discovery URL (_after_ following redirects).

The following is an example discovery document declaring support for version 1 of the module registry protocol:

{
  "modules.v1": "https://modules.example.com/v1/"
}

    {
      "modules.v1": "https://modules.example.com/v1/"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#supported-services)
Supported Services
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At present, the following service identifiers are in use:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#)
    [`login.v1`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#login-v1)
    : [login protocol version 1](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/login)
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#)
    [`modules.v1`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#modules-v1)
    : [module registry API version 1](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/module-registry-protocol)
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#)
    [`providers.v1`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#providers-v1)
    : [provider registry API version 1](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-registry-protocol)
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#authentication)
Authentication
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If credentials for the given hostname are available in [the CLI config](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/config/config-file#Credentials)
 through a `credentials_helper` or a host-specific environment variable, then they will be included in the request for the discovery document.

The credentials may also be provided to endpoints declared in the discovery document, depending on the requirements of the service in question.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/remote-service-discovery#non-standard-ports-in-user-facing-hostnames)
Non-standard Ports in User-facing Hostnames
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It is strongly recommended to provide the discovery document for a hostname on the standard HTTPS port 443. However, in development environments this is not always possible or convenient, so Terraform allows a hostname to end with a port specification consisting of a colon followed by one or more decimal digits.

When a custom port number is present, the service on that port is expected to implement HTTPS and respond to the same fixed discovery path.

For day-to-day use it is strongly recommended _not_ to rely on this mechanism and to instead provide the discovery document on the standard port, since this allows use of the most user-friendly hostname form.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.12.x/docs/internals/remote-service-discovery.mdx)

---

# Collect Provider Metadata | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

Collect Provider Metadata
=========================

This topic describes how to create an inferface in the providers you develop that allows you to collect metadata that is unrelated to the resources in the module, such as usage statistics. This is an advanced topic and is not required to use Terraform.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-meta#introduction)
Introduction
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In some situations it is beneficial for a provider to offer an interface through which modules can pass it information unrelated to the resources in the module, but scoped on a per-module basis.

Provider metadata allows a provider to declare metadata fields it expects, which individual modules can then populate independently of any provider configuration. While provider configurations are often shared between modules, provider metadata is always module-specific.

Provider metadata is intended primarily for the situation where an official module is developed by the same vendor that produced the provider it is intended to work with, to allow the vendor to indirectly obtain usage statistics for each module via the provider. For that reason, this documentation is presented from the perspective of the provider developer rather than the module developer.

**Experimental Feature:** This functionality is experimental. You should [coordinate with the Terraform team](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/new)
 so that they can understand how you are using this functionality. Doing so ensures that your use cases are taken into account as the feature evolves.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-meta#defining-the-schema)
Defining the Schema
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before a provider can receive information from a module, the provider must strictly define the data it can accept. You can do this by setting the `ProviderMeta` property on your `schema.Provider` struct. Its value functions similarly to the provider config: a map of strings to the `schema.Schema` describing the values those strings accept.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-meta#using-the-data)
Using the Data
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When Terraform calls your provider, you can use the `schema.ResourceData` that your `Create`, `Read`, and `Update` functions already use to get access to the provider metadata being passed. First define a struct that matches your schema, then call the `GetProviderSchema` method on your `schema.ResourceData`, passing a pointer to a variable of that type. The variable will be populated with the provider metadata, and will return an error if there was an issue with parsing the data into the struct.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-meta#specifying-data-in-modules)
Specifying Data in Modules
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To include data in your modules, create a `provider_meta` nested block under your module's `terraform` block, with the name of the provider it's trying to pass information to:

    terraform {
      provider_meta "my-provider" {
        hello = "world"
      }
    }
    

The `provider_meta` block must match the schema the provider has defined.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-meta#versioning-your-modules)
Versioning Your Modules
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any module taking advantage of this functionality must make sure that the provider metadata supplied matches the schema defined in the provider, and that the version of Terraform that is being run has support for the provider metadata functionality. It's therefore recommended that any module taking advantage of this functionality should specify a minimum Terraform version of 0.13.0 or higher, and a minimum version of each of the providers it specifies metadata as the first version the schema being used was supported by the provider.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.12.x/docs/internals/provider-meta.mdx)

---

# terraform metadata functions command reference | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

terraform metadata functions command
====================================

The `terraform metadata functions` command prints signatures for the functions available in the current Terraform version.

`terraform metadata functions` requires **Terraform v1.4 or later**.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#usage)
Usage
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Usage: `terraform metadata functions [options]`

The following flags are available:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#)
    [`-json`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#json)
     - Displays the function signatures in a machine-readable, JSON format.

Please note that, at this time, the `-json` flag is a _required_ option. In future releases, this command will be extended to allow for additional options.

The output includes a `format_version` key, which as of Terraform 1.4.0 has value `"1.0"`. The semantics of this version are:

*   We will increment the minor version, e.g. `"1.1"`, for backward-compatible changes or additions. Ignore any object properties with unrecognized names to remain forward-compatible with future minor versions.
*   We will increment the major version, e.g. `"2.0"`, for changes that are not backward-compatible. Reject any input which reports an unsupported major version.

We will introduce new major versions only within the bounds of [the Terraform 1.0 Compatibility Promises](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/v1-compatibility-promises)
.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#format-summary)
Format Summary
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following sections describe the JSON output format by example, using a pseudo-JSON notation. Important elements are described with comments, which are prefixed with `//`. To avoid excessive repetition, we've split the complete format into several discrete sub-objects, described under separate headers. References wrapped in angle brackets (like `<block-representation>`) are placeholders which, in the real output, would be replaced by an instance of the specified sub-object.

The JSON output format consists of the following objects and sub-objects:

*   [Function Signature Representation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#function-signature-representation)
     - the top-level object returned by `terraform metadata functions -json`
*   [Parameter Representation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#parameter-representation)
     - a sub-object of signatures that describes their parameters

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#function-signature-representation)
Function Signature Representation
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    {
      "format_version": "1.0",
    
      // "function_signatures" describes the signatures for all
      // available functions.
      "function_signatures": {
        // keys in this map are the function names, such as "abs"
        "example_function": {
          // "description" is an English-language description of
          // the purpose and usage of the function in Markdown.
          "description": "string",
    
          // "return_type" is a representation of a type specification
          // that the function returns.
          "return_type": "string",
    
          // "parameters" is an optional list of the positional parameters
          // that the function accepts.
          "parameters": [\
            <parameter-representation>,\
            …\
          ],
    
          // "variadic_parameter" is an optional representation of the
          // additional arguments that the function accepts after those
          // matching with the fixed parameters.
          "variadic_parameter": <parameter-representation>
        },
        "example_function_two": { … }
      }
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/functions-meta#parameter-representation)
Parameter Representation
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A parameter representation describes a parameter to a function.

    {
      // "name" is the internal name of the parameter
      "name": "string",
    
      // "description" is an optional English-language description of
      // the purpose and usage of the parameter in Markdown.
      "description": "string",
    
      // "type" is a representation of a type specification
      // that the parameter's value must conform to.
      "type": "string"
    }
    

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.12.x/docs/internals/functions-meta.mdx)

---

# Terraform internals | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

You are viewing documentation for version v1.11.x. [View latest version](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals)
.

Terraform internals overview
============================

This topic provides overview information about the internals of Terraform. Topics in this section explain how Terraform generates plans and describe the provider lifecycle. You do not have to understand Terraform internals to use Terraform.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.11.x/docs/internals/index.mdx)

# Internals | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

You are viewing documentation for version v1.10.x. [View latest version](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals)
.

Terraform Internals
===================

This section covers the internals of Terraform and explains how plans are generated, the lifecycle of a provider, etc. The goal of this section is to remove any notion of "magic" from Terraform. We want you to be able to trust and understand what Terraform is doing to function.

**Note:** Knowledge of Terraform internals is not required to use Terraform. If you aren't interested in the internals of Terraform, you may safely skip this section.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.10.x/docs/internals/index.mdx)

---

# Machine-readable output reference | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

Machine-readable UI Output Reference
====================================

This topic provides reference information about the machine-readable output Terraform prints.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#introduction)
Introduction
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, many Terraform commands display UI output as unstructured text that is intended to be read in a terminal emulator. This text stream is not a stable interface for integrations. Some commands support a `-json` flag, which enables a structured JSON output mode with a defined interface.

For long-running commands such as `plan`, `apply`, `refresh`, and `test`, the `-json` flag outputs a stream of JSON UI messages. The ouptut messages appear one per line so that you can process the messages by integrating software filtering, combining, or modifying the output as desired.

The first message output has type `version`, and includes a `ui` key, which as of Terraform 1.1.0 has value `"1.0"`. The semantics of this version are:

*   We will increment the minor version, e.g. `"1.1"`, for backward-compatible changes or additions. Ignore any object properties with unrecognized names to remain forward-compatible with future minor versions.
*   We will increment the major version, e.g. `"2.0"`, for changes that are not backward-compatible. Reject any input which reports an unsupported major version.

Refer to [Terraform 1.0 Compatibility Promises](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/v1-compatibility-promises)
 for additional information.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#sample-json-output)
Sample JSON Output
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below is sample output from running `terraform apply -json`:

    {"@level":"info","@message":"Terraform 0.15.4","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.275359-04:00","terraform":"0.15.4","type":"version","ui":"0.1.0"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"random_pet.animal: Plan to create","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.705503-04:00","change":{"resource":{"addr":"random_pet.animal","module":"","resource":"random_pet.animal","implied_provider":"random","resource_type":"random_pet","resource_name":"animal","resource_key":null},"action":"create"},"type":"planned_change"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"Plan: 1 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.705638-04:00","changes":{"add":1,"change":0,"remove":0,"operation":"plan"},"type":"change_summary"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"random_pet.animal: Creating...","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.825308-04:00","hook":{"resource":{"addr":"random_pet.animal","module":"","resource":"random_pet.animal","implied_provider":"random","resource_type":"random_pet","resource_name":"animal","resource_key":null},"action":"create"},"type":"apply_start"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"random_pet.animal: Creation complete after 0s [id=smart-lizard]","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.826179-04:00","hook":{"resource":{"addr":"random_pet.animal","module":"","resource":"random_pet.animal","implied_provider":"random","resource_type":"random_pet","resource_name":"animal","resource_key":null},"action":"create","id_key":"id","id_value":"smart-lizard","elapsed_seconds":0},"type":"apply_complete"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"Apply complete! Resources: 1 added, 0 changed, 0 destroyed.","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.869168-04:00","changes":{"add":1,"change":0,"remove":0,"operation":"apply"},"type":"change_summary"}
    {"@level":"info","@message":"Outputs: 1","@module":"terraform.ui","@timestamp":"2021-05-25T13:32:41.869280-04:00","outputs":{"pets":{"sensitive":false,"type":"string","value":"smart-lizard"}},"type":"outputs"}
    

Each line consists of a JSON object with several keys common to all messages. These are:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`@level`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#level)
    : this is normally "info", but can be "error" or "warn" when showing diagnostics
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`@message`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#message)
    : a human-readable summary of the contents of this message
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`@module`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#module)
    : always "terraform.ui" when rendering UI output
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`@timestamp`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#timestamp)
    : an RFC3339 timestamp of when the message was output
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#type)
    : defines which kind of message this is and determines how to interpret other keys which may be present

Clients presenting the logs as a user interface should handle unexpected message types by presenting at least the `@message` field to the user.

Messages will be emitted as events occur to trigger them. This means that messages related to several resources may be interleaved (if Terraform is running with concurrency above 1). The [`resource` object value](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
 can be used to link multiple messages about a single resource.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#message-types)
Message Types
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following message types are supported:

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#generic-messages)
Generic Messages

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#version)
    : information about the Terraform version and the version of the schema used for the following messages
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`log`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#log)
    : unstructured human-readable log lines
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`diagnostic`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#diagnostic)
    : diagnostic warning or error messages; [see the `terraform validate` docs for more details on the format](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/validate#json)
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#operation-results)
Operation Results

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource_drift`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource_drift)
    : describes a detected change to a single resource made outside of Terraform
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`planned_change`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#planned_change)
    : describes a planned change to a single resource
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`change_summary`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#change_summary)
    : summary of all planned or applied changes
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`outputs`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#outputs)
    : list of all root module outputs

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-progress)
Resource Progress

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`apply_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply_start)
    , `apply_progress`, `apply_complete`, `apply_errored`: sequence of messages indicating progress of a single resource through apply
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provision_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision_start)
    , `provision_progress`, `provision_complete`, `provision_errored`: sequence of messages indicating progress of a single provisioner step
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`refresh_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#refresh_start)
    , `refresh_complete`: sequence of messages indicating progress of a single resource through refresh

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test-results)
Test Results

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_abstract`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_abstract)
    : describes the test operation that Terraform executes
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_file`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_file)
    : describes the status of a completed test file
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_run`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_run)
    : describes the status of a completed `run` block within a test file
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_cleanup`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_cleanup)
    : describes the result of the test cleanup after a completed test file
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_summary`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_summary)
    : describes the overall status of the completed test operation
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_plan`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_plan)
    : describes the output of a given `run` block when `command = plan`
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_state`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_state)
    : describes the output of a given `run` block when `command = apply`
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`test_interrupt`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#test_interrupt)
    : describes the result of an interrupted test operation

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#version-message)
Version Message
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A machine-readable UI command output will always begin with a `version` message. The following message-specific keys are defined:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`terraform`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#terraform)
    : the Terraform version which emitted this message
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`ui`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#ui)
    : the machine-readable UI schema version defining the meaning of the following messages

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "Terraform 0.15.4",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.275359-04:00",
      "terraform": "0.15.4",
      "type": "version",
      "ui": "0.1.0"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-drift)
Resource Drift
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If drift is detected during planning, Terraform will emit a `resource_drift` message for each resource which has changed outside of Terraform. This message has an embedded `change` object with the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource)
    : object describing the address of the resource to be changed; see [resource object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
     below for details
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`action`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#action)
    : the action planned to be taken for the resource. Values: `update`, `delete`.

This message does not include details about the exact changes which caused the change to be planned. That information is available in [the JSON plan output](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/json-format)
.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-1)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "random_pet.animal: Drift detected (update)",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.705503-04:00",
      "change": {
        "resource": {
          "addr": "random_pet.animal",
          "module": "",
          "resource": "random_pet.animal",
          "implied_provider": "random",
          "resource_type": "random_pet",
          "resource_name": "animal",
          "resource_key": null
        },
        "action": "update"
      },
      "type": "resource_drift"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#planned-change)
Planned Change
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At the end of a plan or before an apply, Terraform will emit a `planned_change` message for each resource which has changes to apply. This message has an embedded `change` object with the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-1)
    : object describing the address of the resource to be changed; see [resource object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
     below for details
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`previous_resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#previous_resource)
    : object describing the previous address of the resource, if this change includes a configuration-driven move
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`action`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#action-1)
    : the action planned to be taken for the resource. Values: `noop`, `create`, `read`, `update`, `replace`, `delete`, `move`, `import`.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`reason`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#reason)
    : an optional reason for the change, only used when the action is `replace` or `delete`. Values:
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`tainted`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#tainted)
        : resource was marked as tainted
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`requested`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#requested)
        : user requested that the resource be replaced, for example via the `-replace` plan flag
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`cannot_update`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#cannot_update)
        : changes to configuration force the resource to be deleted and created rather than updated
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`delete_because_no_resource_config`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#delete_because_no_resource_config)
        : no matching resource in configuration
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`delete_because_wrong_repetition`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#delete_because_wrong_repetition)
        : resource instance key has no corresponding `count` or `for_each` in configuration
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`delete_because_count_index`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#delete_because_count_index)
        : resource instance key is outside the range of the `count` argument
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`delete_because_each_key`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#delete_because_each_key)
        : resource instance key is not included in the `for_each` argument
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
        [`delete_because_no_module`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#delete_because_no_module)
        : enclosing module instance is not in configuration

This message does not include details about the exact changes which caused the change to be planned. That information is available in [the JSON plan output](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/json-format)
.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-2)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "random_pet.animal: Plan to create",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.705503-04:00",
      "change": {
        "resource": {
          "addr": "random_pet.animal",
          "module": "",
          "resource": "random_pet.animal",
          "implied_provider": "random",
          "resource_type": "random_pet",
          "resource_name": "animal",
          "resource_key": null
        },
        "action": "create"
      },
      "type": "planned_change"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#change-summary)
Change Summary
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Terraform outputs a change summary when a plan or apply operation completes. Both message types include a `changes` object, which has the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`add`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#add)
    : count of resources to be created (including as part of replacement)
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`change`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#change)
    : count of resources to be changed in-place
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`remove`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#remove)
    : count of resources to be destroyed (including as part of replacement)
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`operation`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#operation)
    : one of `plan`, `apply`, or `destroy`

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-3)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "Apply complete! Resources: 1 added, 0 changed, 0 destroyed.",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.869168-04:00",
      "changes": {
        "add": 1,
        "change": 0,
        "remove": 0,
        "operation": "apply"
      },
      "type": "change_summary"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#outputs-1)
Outputs
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After a successful plan or apply, a message with type `outputs` contains the values of all root module output values. This message contains an `outputs` object, the keys of which are the output names. The outputs values are objects with the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`action`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#action-2)
    : for planned outputs, the action which will be taken for the output. Values: `noop`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`value`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#value)
    : for applied outputs, the value of the output, encoded in JSON
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#type-1)
    : for applied outputs, the detected HCL type of the output value
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`sensitive`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#sensitive)
    : boolean value, `true` if the output is sensitive and should be hidden from UI by default

Note that `sensitive` outputs still include the `value` field, and integrating software should respect the sensitivity value as appropriate for the given use case.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-4)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "Outputs: 1",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.869280-04:00",
      "outputs": {
        "pets": {
          "sensitive": false,
          "type": "string",
          "value": "smart-lizard"
        }
      },
      "type": "outputs"
    }
    

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#operation-messages)
Operation Messages
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Performing Terraform operations to a resource will often result in several messages being emitted. The message types include:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`apply_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply_start-1)
    : when starting to apply changes for a resource
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`apply_progress`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply_progress)
    : periodically, showing elapsed time output
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`apply_complete`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply_complete)
    : on successful operation completion
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`apply_errored`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply_errored)
    : when an error is encountered during the operation
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provision_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision_start-1)
    : when starting a provisioner step
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provision_progress`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision_progress)
    : on provisioner output
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provision_complete`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision_complete)
    : on successful provisioning
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provision_errored`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision_errored)
    : when an error is enountered during provisioning
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`refresh_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#refresh_start-1)
    : when reading a resource during refresh
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`refresh_complete`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#refresh_complete)
    : on successful refresh
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`ephemeral_op_start`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#ephemeral_op_start)
    : when starting an ephemeral resource operation
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`ephemeral_op_progress`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#ephemeral_op_progress)
    : periodically showing elapsed time output during ephemeral resource operation
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`ephemeral_op_complete`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#ephemeral_op_complete)
    : on successful ephemeral resource operation completion
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`ephemeral_op_errored`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#ephemeral_op_errored)
    : when an error is encountered during ephemeral resource operation

Each of these messages has a `hook` object, which has different fields for each type. All hooks have a [`resource` object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
 which identifies which resource is the subject of the operation.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply-start)
Apply Start
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `apply_start` message `hook` object has the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-2)
    : a [`resource` object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
     identifying the resource
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`action`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#action-3)
    : the action to be taken for the resource. Values: `noop`, `create`, `read`, `update`, `replace`, `delete`
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`id_key`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#id_key)
     and `id_value`: a key/value pair used to identify this instance of the resource, omitted when unknown

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-5)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "random_pet.animal: Creating...",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-05-25T13:32:41.825308-04:00",

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#apply-progress)
Apply Progress
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `apply_progress` message `hook` object has the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-3)
    : a [`resource` object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)

    [`action`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#action-4)

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`elapsed_seconds`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#elapsed_seconds)
    : time elapsed since the apply operation started, expressed as an integer number of seconds

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-6)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "null_resource.none[4]: Still creating... [30s elapsed]",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-03-17T09:34:26.222465-04:00",
      "hook": {
        "resource": {
          "addr": "null_resource.none[4]",
          "module": "",

          "resource_name": "none",
          "resource_key": 4
        },
        "action": "create",
        "elapsed_seconds": 30
      },
      "type": "apply_progress"
    }

    [`id_key`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#id_key-1)

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`elapsed_seconds`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#elapsed_seconds-1)

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)

          "resource_name": "none",

        "elapsed_seconds": 10

      "type": "apply_errored"

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provision-progress)
Provision Progress
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `provision_progress` message `hook` object has the following keys:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`resource`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-7)
    : a [`resource` object](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#resource-object)
     identifying the resource
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`provisioner`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#provisioner-1)
    : the type of provisioner
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#)
    [`output`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#output)
    : the output log from the provisioner

One `provision_progress` message is output for each log line received from the provisioner.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/machine-readable-ui#example-10)
Example

    {
      "@level": "info",
      "@message": "null_resource.none[0]: (local-exec): Executing: [\"/bin/sh\" \"-c\" \"sleep 10 && exit 1\"]",
      "@module": "terraform.ui",
      "@timestamp": "2021-03-26T16:38:43.997869-04:00",
      "hook": {
        "resource": {
          "addr": "null_resource.none[0]",
          "module": "",
          "resource": "null_resource.none[0]",
          "implied_provider": "null",
          "resource_type": "null_resource",
          "resource_name": "none",
          "resource_key": 0
        },
        "provisioner": "local-exec",
        "output": "Executing: [\"/bin/sh\" \"-c\" \"sleep 10 && exit 1\"]"
      },
      "type": "provision_progress"
    }
