*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#)
    
    [`url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#url)
     (required): a string specifying the URL from which Terraform should download the `.zip` archive containing the requested provider plugin version.
    
    Terraform resolves the URL relative to the URL from which the current JSON document was returned, so the examples above containing only a filename would cause Terraform to construct a URL like:
    
        https://terraform.example.com/providers/registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/random/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_darwin_amd64.zip
        
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#)
    
    [`hashes`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#hashes)
     (optional): a JSON array of strings containing one or more hash values for the indicated archive. These hashes use Terraform's provider package hashing algorithm. At present, the easiest way to populate these is to construct a mirror's JSON indices using the `terraform providers mirror` command, as described in a later section, which will include the calculated hashes of each provider.
    
    If the response includes at least one hash, Terraform will select the hash whose algorithm it considers to be strongest and verify that the downloaded package matches that hash. If the response does not include a `hashes` property then Terraform will install the indicated archive with no verification.
    

Terraform CLI will only attempt to download versions that it has previously seen in response to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#list-available-versions)
.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.13.x/provider-network-mirror-protocol#provider-mirror-as-a-static-website)
Provider Mirror as a Static Website
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The provider mirror protocol is designed so that it can potentially be implemented by placing files on typical static website hosting services. When using this strategy, implement the JSON index responses described above as `.json` files in the appropriate nested subdirectories, and ensure that your system is configured to serve `.json` files with the `application/json` media type.

As a convenience, Terraform CLI includes [the `terraform providers mirror` subcommand](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.13.x%20(beta)/commands/providers/mirror)
, which will analyze the current configuration for the providers it requires, download the packages for those providers from their origin registries, and place them into a local directory suitable for use as a mirror.

The `terraform providers mirror` subcommand also generates `index.json` and version-specific `.json` files that can, when placed in a static website hosting system, produce responses compatible with the provider mirror protocol.

If you wish to create a mirror with providers for a number of different Terraform configurations, run `terraform providers mirror` in each configuration in turn while providing the same output directory each time. Terraform will then merge together all of the requirements into a single set of JSON indices.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.13.x/docs/internals/provider-network-mirror-protocol.mdx)

---

# Provider Registry Protocol | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

You are viewing documentation for version v1.9.x. [View latest version](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-registry-protocol)
.

Provider Registry Protocol
==========================

Third-party provider registries are supported only in Terraform CLI 0.13 and later. Prior versions do not support this protocol.

The provider registry protocol is what Terraform CLI uses to discover metadata about providers available for installation and to locate the distribution packages for a selected provider.

The primary implementation of this protocol is the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
 at `registry.terraform.io`. By writing and deploying your own implementation of this protocol, you can create a separate _origin registry_ to distribute your own providers, as an alternative to publishing them on the public Terraform Registry.

This page describes the provider _registry_ protocol, which is the protocol for finding providers available for installation. It _doesn't_ describe the API that provider plugins themselves implement to serve requests from Terraform CLI at runtime. For more information on the provider API, see the Terraform SDK documentation.

The public Terraform Registry implements a superset of the API described on this page, in order to capture additional information used in the registry UI. Third-party implementations should not include those extensions because they may change in future without notice.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-addresses)
Provider Addresses
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each Terraform provider has an associated address which uniquely identifies it within Terraform. A provider address has the syntax `hostname/namespace/type`, where:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hostname`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#hostname)
     is the registry host that the provider is considered to have originated from, and the default location Terraform will consult for information about the provider [unless overridden in the CLI configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.9.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace)
     is the name of a namespace, unique on a particular hostname, that can contain one or more providers that are somehow related. On the public Terraform Registry the "namespace" represents the organization that is packaging and distributing the provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#type)
     is the provider type, like "azurerm", "aws", "google", "dns", etc. A provider type is unique within a particular hostname and namespace.

The `hostname/` portion of a provider address (including its slash delimiter) is optional, and if omitted defaults to `registry.terraform.io/`.

For example:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hashicorp/aws`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#hashicorp-aws)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws`, which is the official AWS provider published by HashiCorp.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example/foo`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-foo)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/example/foo`, which is a hypothetical third-party provider published on the public Terraform Registry.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example.com/bar/baz`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-com-bar-baz)
     is a hypothetical third-party provider published at a third-party provider registry on `example.com`.

If you intend only to share a provider you've developed for use by all Terraform users, please consider publishing it into the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
, which will make your provider discoverable. You only need to implement this provider registry protocol if you wish to publish providers whose addresses include a different hostname that is under your control.

Terraform uses the full address (after normalization to always include a hostname) as its global identifier for providers internally, and so it's important to note that re-uploading the `hashicorp/azurerm` provider into another namespace or publishing it on a different hostname will cause Terraform to see it as an entirely separate provider that will _not_ be usable by modules that declare a dependency on `hashicorp/azurerm`. If your goal is to create an alternative local distribution source for an existing provider -- that is, a _mirror_ of the provider -- refer to [the provider installation method configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.9.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
 instead.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-versions)
Provider Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each distinct provider address has associated with it a set of versions, each of which has an associated version number. Terraform assumes version numbers follow the [Semantic Versioning 2.0](https://semver.org/)
 conventions, with the schema and behavior of the provider as documented from the perspective of an end-user of Terraform serving as the "public API".

All available versions for a particular provider address are considered to be the same provider by Terraform. Each Terraform configuration selects only one version of each provider for use in the entire configuration, so the version constraints across all modules are considered together for the purposes of version selection.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#service-discovery)
Service Discovery
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The providers protocol begins with Terraform CLI using [Terraform's remote service discovery protocol](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/remote-service-discovery)
, with the hostname in the provider address acting as the "User-facing Hostname".

The service identifier for the provider registry protocol is `providers.v1`. Its associated string value is the base URL for the relative URLs defined in the sections that follow.

For example, the service discovery document for a host that _only_ implements the provider registry protocol might contain the following:

    {
      "providers.v1": "/terraform/providers/v1/"
    }
    

If the given URL is a relative URL then Terraform will interpret it as relative to the discovery document itself. The specific provider registry protocol endpoints are defined as URLs relative to the given base URL, and so the specified base URL should generally end with a slash to ensure that those relative paths will be resolved as expected.

The following sections describe the various operations that a provider registry must implement to be compatible with Terraform CLI's provider installer. The indicated URLs are all relative to the URL resulting from service discovery, as described above. We use the current URLs on Terraform Registry as working examples, assuming that the caller already performed service discovery on `registry.terraform.io` to learn the base URL.

The URLs are shown with the convention that a path portion with a colon `:` prefix is a placeholder for a dynamically-selected value, while all other path portions are literal. For example, in `:namespace/:type/versions`, the first two path portions are placeholders while the third is literally the string "versions".

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
List Available Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation determines which versions are currently available for a particular provider.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/versions` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-1)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-1)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/versions'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response)
Sample Response

    {
      "versions": [\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.0",\
          "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        },\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.1",\
          "protocols": ["5.2"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        }\
      ]
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object containing a single property `versions`. `versions` is an array of objects that each describe one available version, with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#version)
     (required): the version number this object is describing, using the semantic versioning string notation. `version` must be unique across all objects in the response.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols)
     (recommended): an array of Terraform provider API versions that this version supports, each given in `MAJOR.MINOR` format where each major version appears only once and the given minor version is the highest minor version supported. For example, `5.1` means that the provider supports both protocol `5.0` and protocol `5.1`.
    
    Terraform uses this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider to work with their current version of Terraform, if their currently-selected versions are not compatible.
    
    Which API versions are supported is, for most providers, decided by which version of the Terraform SDK they are built against. Consult the Terraform SDK documentation for more information.
    
    Only Terraform 0.13 and later support third-party provider registries and that Terraform version requires API version `5.0` or later, so in practice it isn't useful to list major versions 4 or earlier in a third-party provider registry.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`platforms`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#platforms)
     (recommended): an array of objects describing platforms that have packages available for this version.
    
    Terraform may use this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider for compatibility with their current platform.
    
    The `platforms` objects have properties `os` and `arch`, whose values match the properties of the same name in the response to [Find a Provider Package](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
    .
    

Return `404 Not Found` to signal that the registry does not have a provider with the given namespace and type.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
Find a Provider Package
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation returns the download URL of and associated metadata about the distribution package for a particular version of a provider for a particular operating system and architecture.

Terraform CLI uses this operation after it has selected the newest available version matching the configured version constraints, in order to find the zip archive containing the plugin itself.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/:version/download/:os/:arch` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters-1)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-2)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-2)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#version-1)
     (required): the version selected to download. This will exactly match one of the version strings returned from a previous call to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#os)
     (required): a keyword identifying the operating system that the returned package should be compatible with, like "linux" or "darwin".
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch)
     (required): a keyword identifying the CPU architecture that the returned package should be compatible with, like "amd64" or "arm".

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request-1)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/2.0.0/download/linux/amd64'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response-1)
Sample Response

    {
      "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],
      "os": "linux",
      "arch": "amd64",
      "filename": "terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "download_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "shasums_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS",
      "shasums_signature_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS.sig",
      "shasum": "5f9c7aa76b7c34d722fc9123208e26b22d60440cb47150dd04733b9b94f4541a",
      "signing_keys": {
        "gpg_public_keys": [\
          {\
            "key_id": "51852D87348FFC4C",\
            "ascii_armor": "-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----\nVersion: GnuPG v1\n\nmQENBFMORM0BCADBRyKO1MhCirazOSVwcfTr1xUxjPvfxD3hjUwHtjsOy/bT6p9f\nW2mRPfwnq2JB5As+paL3UGDsSRDnK9KAxQb0NNF4+eVhr/EJ18s3wwXXDMjpIifq\nfIm2WyH3G+aRLTLPIpscUNKDyxFOUbsmgXAmJ46Re1fn8uKxKRHbfa39aeuEYWFA\n3drdL1WoUngvED7f+RnKBK2G6ZEpO+LDovQk19xGjiMTtPJrjMjZJ3QXqPvx5wca\nKSZLr4lMTuoTI/ZXyZy5bD4tShiZz6KcyX27cD70q2iRcEZ0poLKHyEIDAi3TM5k\nSwbbWBFd5RNPOR0qzrb/0p9ksKK48IIfH2FvABEBAAG0K0hhc2hpQ29ycCBTZWN1\ncml0eSA8c2VjdXJpdHlAaGFzaGljb3JwLmNvbT6JATgEEwECACIFAlMORM0CGwMG\nCwkIBwMCBhUIAgkKCwQWAgMBAh4BAheAAAoJEFGFLYc0j/xMyWIIAIPhcVqiQ59n\nJc07gjUX0SWBJAxEG1lKxfzS4Xp+57h2xxTpdotGQ1fZwsihaIqow337YHQI3q0i\nSqV534Ms+j/tU7X8sq11xFJIeEVG8PASRCwmryUwghFKPlHETQ8jJ+Y8+1asRydi\npsP3B/5Mjhqv/uOK+Vy3zAyIpyDOMtIpOVfjSpCplVRdtSTFWBu9Em7j5I2HMn1w\nsJZnJgXKpybpibGiiTtmnFLOwibmprSu04rsnP4ncdC2XRD4wIjoyA+4PKgX3sCO\nklEzKryWYBmLkJOMDdo52LttP3279s7XrkLEE7ia0fXa2c12EQ0f0DQ1tGUvyVEW\nWmJVccm5bq25AQ0EUw5EzQEIANaPUY04/g7AmYkOMjaCZ6iTp9hB5Rsj/4ee/ln9\nwArzRO9+3eejLWh53FoN1rO+su7tiXJA5YAzVy6tuolrqjM8DBztPxdLBbEi4V+j\n2tK0dATdBQBHEh3OJApO2UBtcjaZBT31zrG9K55D+CrcgIVEHAKY8Cb4kLBkb5wM\nskn+DrASKU0BNIV1qRsxfiUdQHZfSqtp004nrql1lbFMLFEuiY8FZrkkQ9qduixo\nmTT6f34/oiY+Jam3zCK7RDN/OjuWheIPGj/Qbx9JuNiwgX6yRj7OE1tjUx6d8g9y\n0H1fmLJbb3WZZbuuGFnK6qrE3bGeY8+AWaJAZ37wpWh1p0cAEQEAAYkBHwQYAQIA\nCQUCUw5EzQIbDAAKCRBRhS2HNI/8TJntCAClU7TOO/X053eKF1jqNW4A1qpxctVc\nz8eTcY8Om5O4f6a/rfxfNFKn9Qyja/OG1xWNobETy7MiMXYjaa8uUx5iFy6kMVaP\n0BXJ59NLZjMARGw6lVTYDTIvzqqqwLxgliSDfSnqUhubGwvykANPO+93BBx89MRG\nunNoYGXtPlhNFrAsB1VR8+EyKLv2HQtGCPSFBhrjuzH3gxGibNDDdFQLxxuJWepJ\nEK1UbTS4ms0NgZ2Uknqn1WRU1Ki7rE4sTy68iZtWpKQXZEJa0IGnuI2sSINGcXCJ\noEIgXTMyCILo34Fa/C6VCm2WBgz9zZO8/rHIiQm1J5zqz0DrDwKBUM9C\n=LYpS\n-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----",\
            "trust_signature": "",\
            "source": "HashiCorp",\
            "source_url": "https://www.hashicorp.com/security.html"\
          }\
        ]
      }
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties-1)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols-1)
     (required): an array of Terraform provider API versions that the provider supports, in the same format as for [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
    
    While this property is optional when listing available options, it is _required_ for describing an individual provider package so that Terraform CLI can avoid downloading a package that will not be compatible with it.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#os-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `os` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `arch` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`filename`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#filename)
     (required): the filename for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the "shasums" document, so that Terraform CLI can determine which of the given checksums should be used for this specific package.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`download_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#download_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve the provider's zip archive. If this is a relative URL then it will be resolved relative to the URL that returned the containing JSON object.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a text document recording expected SHA256 checksums for this package and possibly other packages for the same provider version on other platforms.
    
    The indicated document must be in the format generated by the `sha256` command available on many Unix systems, with one entry recording the same filename given in the `filename` property (case sensitive).
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_signature_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_signature_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a binary, detached GPG signature for the document at `shasums_url`, signed by one of the keys indicated in the `signing_keys` property.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasum`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasum)
     (required): the SHA256 checksum for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the shasums document.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`signing_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#signing_keys)
     (required): an object describing signing keys for this provider package, one of which must have been used to produce the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. The object has the following nested properties:
    
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
        
        [`gpg_public_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#gpg_public_keys)
         (required): an array of objects, each describing one GPG signing key that is allowed to sign the checksums for this provider version. At least one element must be included, representing the key that produced the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. These objects have the following nested properties:
        
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`key_id`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#key_id)
             (required): uppercase-hexadecimal-formatted ID for this GPG key
            
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`ascii_armor`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#ascii_armor)
             (required): an "ascii-armor" encoding of the **public key** associated with this GPG key.
            

Return `404 Not Found` to signal that the given provider version isn't available for the requested operating system and/or architecture. Terraform CLI will only attempt to download versions that it has previously seen in response to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.9.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.9.x/docs/internals/provider-registry-protocol.mdx)

---

# Provider Registry Protocol | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

You are viewing documentation for version v1.8.x. [View latest version](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-registry-protocol)
.

Provider Registry Protocol
==========================

Third-party provider registries are supported only in Terraform CLI 0.13 and later. Prior versions do not support this protocol.

The provider registry protocol is what Terraform CLI uses to discover metadata about providers available for installation and to locate the distribution packages for a selected provider.

The primary implementation of this protocol is the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
 at `registry.terraform.io`. By writing and deploying your own implementation of this protocol, you can create a separate _origin registry_ to distribute your own providers, as an alternative to publishing them on the public Terraform Registry.

This page describes the provider _registry_ protocol, which is the protocol for finding providers available for installation. It _doesn't_ describe the API that provider plugins themselves implement to serve requests from Terraform CLI at runtime. For more information on the provider API, see the Terraform SDK documentation.

The public Terraform Registry implements a superset of the API described on this page, in order to capture additional information used in the registry UI. Third-party implementations should not include those extensions because they may change in future without notice.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-addresses)
Provider Addresses
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each Terraform provider has an associated address which uniquely identifies it within Terraform. A provider address has the syntax `hostname/namespace/type`, where:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hostname`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#hostname)
     is the registry host that the provider is considered to have originated from, and the default location Terraform will consult for information about the provider [unless overridden in the CLI configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.8.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace)
     is the name of a namespace, unique on a particular hostname, that can contain one or more providers that are somehow related. On the public Terraform Registry the "namespace" represents the organization that is packaging and distributing the provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#type)
     is the provider type, like "azurerm", "aws", "google", "dns", etc. A provider type is unique within a particular hostname and namespace.

The `hostname/` portion of a provider address (including its slash delimiter) is optional, and if omitted defaults to `registry.terraform.io/`.

For example:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hashicorp/aws`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#hashicorp-aws)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws`, which is the official AWS provider published by HashiCorp.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example/foo`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-foo)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/example/foo`, which is a hypothetical third-party provider published on the public Terraform Registry.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example.com/bar/baz`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-com-bar-baz)
     is a hypothetical third-party provider published at a third-party provider registry on `example.com`.

If you intend only to share a provider you've developed for use by all Terraform users, please consider publishing it into the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
, which will make your provider discoverable. You only need to implement this provider registry protocol if you wish to publish providers whose addresses include a different hostname that is under your control.

Terraform uses the full address (after normalization to always include a hostname) as its global identifier for providers internally, and so it's important to note that re-uploading the `hashicorp/azurerm` provider into another namespace or publishing it on a different hostname will cause Terraform to see it as an entirely separate provider that will _not_ be usable by modules that declare a dependency on `hashicorp/azurerm`. If your goal is to create an alternative local distribution source for an existing provider -- that is, a _mirror_ of the provider -- refer to [the provider installation method configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.8.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
 instead.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-versions)
Provider Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each distinct provider address has associated with it a set of versions, each of which has an associated version number. Terraform assumes version numbers follow the [Semantic Versioning 2.0](https://semver.org/)
 conventions, with the schema and behavior of the provider as documented from the perspective of an end-user of Terraform serving as the "public API".

All available versions for a particular provider address are considered to be the same provider by Terraform. Each Terraform configuration selects only one version of each provider for use in the entire configuration, so the version constraints across all modules are considered together for the purposes of version selection.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#service-discovery)
Service Discovery
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The providers protocol begins with Terraform CLI using [Terraform's remote service discovery protocol](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/remote-service-discovery)
, with the hostname in the provider address acting as the "User-facing Hostname".

The service identifier for the provider registry protocol is `providers.v1`. Its associated string value is the base URL for the relative URLs defined in the sections that follow.

For example, the service discovery document for a host that _only_ implements the provider registry protocol might contain the following:

    {
      "providers.v1": "/terraform/providers/v1/"
    }
    

If the given URL is a relative URL then Terraform will interpret it as relative to the discovery document itself. The specific provider registry protocol endpoints are defined as URLs relative to the given base URL, and so the specified base URL should generally end with a slash to ensure that those relative paths will be resolved as expected.

The following sections describe the various operations that a provider registry must implement to be compatible with Terraform CLI's provider installer. The indicated URLs are all relative to the URL resulting from service discovery, as described above. We use the current URLs on Terraform Registry as working examples, assuming that the caller already performed service discovery on `registry.terraform.io` to learn the base URL.

The URLs are shown with the convention that a path portion with a colon `:` prefix is a placeholder for a dynamically-selected value, while all other path portions are literal. For example, in `:namespace/:type/versions`, the first two path portions are placeholders while the third is literally the string "versions".

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
List Available Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation determines which versions are currently available for a particular provider.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/versions` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-1)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-1)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/versions'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response)
Sample Response

    {
      "versions": [\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.0",\
          "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        },\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.1",\
          "protocols": ["5.2"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        }\
      ]
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object containing a single property `versions`. `versions` is an array of objects that each describe one available version, with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#version)
     (required): the version number this object is describing, using the semantic versioning string notation. `version` must be unique across all objects in the response.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols)
     (recommended): an array of Terraform provider API versions that this version supports, each given in `MAJOR.MINOR` format where each major version appears only once and the given minor version is the highest minor version supported. For example, `5.1` means that the provider supports both protocol `5.0` and protocol `5.1`.
    
    Terraform uses this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider to work with their current version of Terraform, if their currently-selected versions are not compatible.
    
    Which API versions are supported is, for most providers, decided by which version of the Terraform SDK they are built against. Consult the Terraform SDK documentation for more information.
    
    Only Terraform 0.13 and later support third-party provider registries and that Terraform version requires API version `5.0` or later, so in practice it isn't useful to list major versions 4 or earlier in a third-party provider registry.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`platforms`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#platforms)
     (recommended): an array of objects describing platforms that have packages available for this version.
    
    Terraform may use this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider for compatibility with their current platform.
    
    The `platforms` objects have properties `os` and `arch`, whose values match the properties of the same name in the response to [Find a Provider Package](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
    .
    

Return `404 Not Found` to signal that the registry does not have a provider with the given namespace and type.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
Find a Provider Package
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation returns the download URL of and associated metadata about the distribution package for a particular version of a provider for a particular operating system and architecture.

Terraform CLI uses this operation after it has selected the newest available version matching the configured version constraints, in order to find the zip archive containing the plugin itself.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/:version/download/:os/:arch` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters-1)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-2)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-2)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#version-1)
     (required): the version selected to download. This will exactly match one of the version strings returned from a previous call to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#os)
     (required): a keyword identifying the operating system that the returned package should be compatible with, like "linux" or "darwin".
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch)
     (required): a keyword identifying the CPU architecture that the returned package should be compatible with, like "amd64" or "arm".

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request-1)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/2.0.0/download/linux/amd64'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response-1)
Sample Response

    {
      "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],
      "os": "linux",
      "arch": "amd64",
      "filename": "terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "download_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "shasums_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS",
      "shasums_signature_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS.sig",
      "shasum": "5f9c7aa76b7c34d722fc9123208e26b22d60440cb47150dd04733b9b94f4541a",
      "signing_keys": {
        "gpg_public_keys": [\
          {\
            "key_id": "51852D87348FFC4C",\
            "ascii_armor": "-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----\nVersion: GnuPG v1\n\nmQENBFMORM0BCADBRyKO1MhCirazOSVwcfTr1xUxjPvfxD3hjUwHtjsOy/bT6p9f\nW2mRPfwnq2JB5As+paL3UGDsSRDnK9KAxQb0NNF4+eVhr/EJ18s3wwXXDMjpIifq\nfIm2WyH3G+aRLTLPIpscUNKDyxFOUbsmgXAmJ46Re1fn8uKxKRHbfa39aeuEYWFA\n3drdL1WoUngvED7f+RnKBK2G6ZEpO+LDovQk19xGjiMTtPJrjMjZJ3QXqPvx5wca\nKSZLr4lMTuoTI/ZXyZy5bD4tShiZz6KcyX27cD70q2iRcEZ0poLKHyEIDAi3TM5k\nSwbbWBFd5RNPOR0qzrb/0p9ksKK48IIfH2FvABEBAAG0K0hhc2hpQ29ycCBTZWN1\ncml0eSA8c2VjdXJpdHlAaGFzaGljb3JwLmNvbT6JATgEEwECACIFAlMORM0CGwMG\nCwkIBwMCBhUIAgkKCwQWAgMBAh4BAheAAAoJEFGFLYc0j/xMyWIIAIPhcVqiQ59n\nJc07gjUX0SWBJAxEG1lKxfzS4Xp+57h2xxTpdotGQ1fZwsihaIqow337YHQI3q0i\nSqV534Ms+j/tU7X8sq11xFJIeEVG8PASRCwmryUwghFKPlHETQ8jJ+Y8+1asRydi\npsP3B/5Mjhqv/uOK+Vy3zAyIpyDOMtIpOVfjSpCplVRdtSTFWBu9Em7j5I2HMn1w\nsJZnJgXKpybpibGiiTtmnFLOwibmprSu04rsnP4ncdC2XRD4wIjoyA+4PKgX3sCO\nklEzKryWYBmLkJOMDdo52LttP3279s7XrkLEE7ia0fXa2c12EQ0f0DQ1tGUvyVEW\nWmJVccm5bq25AQ0EUw5EzQEIANaPUY04/g7AmYkOMjaCZ6iTp9hB5Rsj/4ee/ln9\nwArzRO9+3eejLWh53FoN1rO+su7tiXJA5YAzVy6tuolrqjM8DBztPxdLBbEi4V+j\n2tK0dATdBQBHEh3OJApO2UBtcjaZBT31zrG9K55D+CrcgIVEHAKY8Cb4kLBkb5wM\nskn+DrASKU0BNIV1qRsxfiUdQHZfSqtp004nrql1lbFMLFEuiY8FZrkkQ9qduixo\nmTT6f34/oiY+Jam3zCK7RDN/OjuWheIPGj/Qbx9JuNiwgX6yRj7OE1tjUx6d8g9y\n0H1fmLJbb3WZZbuuGFnK6qrE3bGeY8+AWaJAZ37wpWh1p0cAEQEAAYkBHwQYAQIA\nCQUCUw5EzQIbDAAKCRBRhS2HNI/8TJntCAClU7TOO/X053eKF1jqNW4A1qpxctVc\nz8eTcY8Om5O4f6a/rfxfNFKn9Qyja/OG1xWNobETy7MiMXYjaa8uUx5iFy6kMVaP\n0BXJ59NLZjMARGw6lVTYDTIvzqqqwLxgliSDfSnqUhubGwvykANPO+93BBx89MRG\nunNoYGXtPlhNFrAsB1VR8+EyKLv2HQtGCPSFBhrjuzH3gxGibNDDdFQLxxuJWepJ\nEK1UbTS4ms0NgZ2Uknqn1WRU1Ki7rE4sTy68iZtWpKQXZEJa0IGnuI2sSINGcXCJ\noEIgXTMyCILo34Fa/C6VCm2WBgz9zZO8/rHIiQm1J5zqz0DrDwKBUM9C\n=LYpS\n-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----",\
            "trust_signature": "",\
            "source": "HashiCorp",\
            "source_url": "https://www.hashicorp.com/security.html"\
          }\
        ]
      }
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties-1)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols-1)
     (required): an array of Terraform provider API versions that the provider supports, in the same format as for [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
    
    While this property is optional when listing available options, it is _required_ for describing an individual provider package so that Terraform CLI can avoid downloading a package that will not be compatible with it.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#os-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `os` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `arch` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`filename`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#filename)
     (required): the filename for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the "shasums" document, so that Terraform CLI can determine which of the given checksums should be used for this specific package.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`download_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#download_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve the provider's zip archive. If this is a relative URL then it will be resolved relative to the URL that returned the containing JSON object.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a text document recording expected SHA256 checksums for this package and possibly other packages for the same provider version on other platforms.
    
    The indicated document must be in the format generated by the `sha256` command available on many Unix systems, with one entry recording the same filename given in the `filename` property (case sensitive).
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_signature_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_signature_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a binary, detached GPG signature for the document at `shasums_url`, signed by one of the keys indicated in the `signing_keys` property.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasum`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasum)
     (required): the SHA256 checksum for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the shasums document.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`signing_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#signing_keys)
     (required): an object describing signing keys for this provider package, one of which must have been used to produce the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. The object has the following nested properties:
    
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
        
        [`gpg_public_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#gpg_public_keys)
         (required): an array of objects, each describing one GPG signing key that is allowed to sign the checksums for this provider version. At least one element must be included, representing the key that produced the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. These objects have the following nested properties:
        
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`key_id`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#key_id)
             (required): uppercase-hexadecimal-formatted ID for this GPG key
            
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`ascii_armor`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#ascii_armor)
             (required): an "ascii-armor" encoding of the **public key** associated with this GPG key.
            

Return `404 Not Found` to signal that the given provider version isn't available for the requested operating system and/or architecture. Terraform CLI will only attempt to download versions that it has previously seen in response to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.8.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
.

[Edit this page on GitHub](https://github.com/hashicorp/web-unified-docs/blob/main/content/terraform/v1.8.x/docs/internals/provider-registry-protocol.mdx)

---

# Provider Registry Protocol | Terraform | HashiCorp Developer

[HashiConf More sessions have been added to the conference agenda. Buy your pass and plan your schedule. Register](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/conferences/hashiconf/register)

Dismiss alert

[Terraform Home](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform)

You are viewing documentation for version v1.7.x. [View latest version](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/provider-registry-protocol)
.

Provider Registry Protocol
==========================

Third-party provider registries are supported only in Terraform CLI 0.13 and later. Prior versions do not support this protocol.

The provider registry protocol is what Terraform CLI uses to discover metadata about providers available for installation and to locate the distribution packages for a selected provider.

The primary implementation of this protocol is the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
 at `registry.terraform.io`. By writing and deploying your own implementation of this protocol, you can create a separate _origin registry_ to distribute your own providers, as an alternative to publishing them on the public Terraform Registry.

This page describes the provider _registry_ protocol, which is the protocol for finding providers available for installation. It _doesn't_ describe the API that provider plugins themselves implement to serve requests from Terraform CLI at runtime. For more information on the provider API, see the Terraform SDK documentation.

The public Terraform Registry implements a superset of the API described on this page, in order to capture additional information used in the registry UI. Third-party implementations should not include those extensions because they may change in future without notice.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-addresses)
Provider Addresses
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each Terraform provider has an associated address which uniquely identifies it within Terraform. A provider address has the syntax `hostname/namespace/type`, where:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hostname`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#hostname)
     is the registry host that the provider is considered to have originated from, and the default location Terraform will consult for information about the provider [unless overridden in the CLI configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.7.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace)
     is the name of a namespace, unique on a particular hostname, that can contain one or more providers that are somehow related. On the public Terraform Registry the "namespace" represents the organization that is packaging and distributing the provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#type)
     is the provider type, like "azurerm", "aws", "google", "dns", etc. A provider type is unique within a particular hostname and namespace.

The `hostname/` portion of a provider address (including its slash delimiter) is optional, and if omitted defaults to `registry.terraform.io/`.

For example:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`hashicorp/aws`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#hashicorp-aws)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws`, which is the official AWS provider published by HashiCorp.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example/foo`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-foo)
     is a shorthand for `registry.terraform.io/example/foo`, which is a hypothetical third-party provider published on the public Terraform Registry.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`example.com/bar/baz`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#example-com-bar-baz)
     is a hypothetical third-party provider published at a third-party provider registry on `example.com`.

If you intend only to share a provider you've developed for use by all Terraform users, please consider publishing it into the public [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/)
, which will make your provider discoverable. You only need to implement this provider registry protocol if you wish to publish providers whose addresses include a different hostname that is under your control.

Terraform uses the full address (after normalization to always include a hostname) as its global identifier for providers internally, and so it's important to note that re-uploading the `hashicorp/azurerm` provider into another namespace or publishing it on a different hostname will cause Terraform to see it as an entirely separate provider that will _not_ be usable by modules that declare a dependency on `hashicorp/azurerm`. If your goal is to create an alternative local distribution source for an existing provider -- that is, a _mirror_ of the provider -- refer to [the provider installation method configuration](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/v1.7.x/config/config-file#provider-installation)
 instead.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#provider-versions)
Provider Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each distinct provider address has associated with it a set of versions, each of which has an associated version number. Terraform assumes version numbers follow the [Semantic Versioning 2.0](https://semver.org/)
 conventions, with the schema and behavior of the provider as documented from the perspective of an end-user of Terraform serving as the "public API".

All available versions for a particular provider address are considered to be the same provider by Terraform. Each Terraform configuration selects only one version of each provider for use in the entire configuration, so the version constraints across all modules are considered together for the purposes of version selection.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#service-discovery)
Service Discovery
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The providers protocol begins with Terraform CLI using [Terraform's remote service discovery protocol](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/remote-service-discovery)
, with the hostname in the provider address acting as the "User-facing Hostname".

The service identifier for the provider registry protocol is `providers.v1`. Its associated string value is the base URL for the relative URLs defined in the sections that follow.

For example, the service discovery document for a host that _only_ implements the provider registry protocol might contain the following:

    {
      "providers.v1": "/terraform/providers/v1/"
    }
    

If the given URL is a relative URL then Terraform will interpret it as relative to the discovery document itself. The specific provider registry protocol endpoints are defined as URLs relative to the given base URL, and so the specified base URL should generally end with a slash to ensure that those relative paths will be resolved as expected.

The following sections describe the various operations that a provider registry must implement to be compatible with Terraform CLI's provider installer. The indicated URLs are all relative to the URL resulting from service discovery, as described above. We use the current URLs on Terraform Registry as working examples, assuming that the caller already performed service discovery on `registry.terraform.io` to learn the base URL.

The URLs are shown with the convention that a path portion with a colon `:` prefix is a placeholder for a dynamically-selected value, while all other path portions are literal. For example, in `:namespace/:type/versions`, the first two path portions are placeholders while the third is literally the string "versions".

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
List Available Versions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation determines which versions are currently available for a particular provider.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/versions` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-1)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-1)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/versions'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response)
Sample Response

    {
      "versions": [\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.0",\
          "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        },\
        {\
          "version": "2.0.1",\
          "protocols": ["5.2"],\
          "platforms": [\
            {"os": "darwin", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "amd64"},\
            {"os": "linux", "arch": "arm"},\
            {"os": "windows", "arch": "amd64"}\
          ]\
        }\
      ]
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object containing a single property `versions`. `versions` is an array of objects that each describe one available version, with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#version)
     (required): the version number this object is describing, using the semantic versioning string notation. `version` must be unique across all objects in the response.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols)
     (recommended): an array of Terraform provider API versions that this version supports, each given in `MAJOR.MINOR` format where each major version appears only once and the given minor version is the highest minor version supported. For example, `5.1` means that the provider supports both protocol `5.0` and protocol `5.1`.
    
    Terraform uses this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider to work with their current version of Terraform, if their currently-selected versions are not compatible.
    
    Which API versions are supported is, for most providers, decided by which version of the Terraform SDK they are built against. Consult the Terraform SDK documentation for more information.
    
    Only Terraform 0.13 and later support third-party provider registries and that Terraform version requires API version `5.0` or later, so in practice it isn't useful to list major versions 4 or earlier in a third-party provider registry.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`platforms`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#platforms)
     (recommended): an array of objects describing platforms that have packages available for this version.
    
    Terraform may use this information, when available, to provide hints to users about upgrading or downgrading their version of a particular provider for compatibility with their current platform.
    
    The `platforms` objects have properties `os` and `arch`, whose values match the properties of the same name in the response to [Find a Provider Package](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
    .
    

Return `404 Not Found` to signal that the registry does not have a provider with the given namespace and type.

[](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#find-a-provider-package)
Find a Provider Package
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operation returns the download URL of and associated metadata about the distribution package for a particular version of a provider for a particular operating system and architecture.

Terraform CLI uses this operation after it has selected the newest available version matching the configured version constraints, in order to find the zip archive containing the plugin itself.

| Method | Path | Produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `:namespace/:type/:version/download/:os/:arch` | `application/json` |

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#parameters-1)
Parameters

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`namespace`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#namespace-2)
     (required): the namespace portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`type`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#type-2)
     (required): the type portion of the address of the requested provider.
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`version`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#version-1)
     (required): the version selected to download. This will exactly match one of the version strings returned from a previous call to [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#os)
     (required): a keyword identifying the operating system that the returned package should be compatible with, like "linux" or "darwin".
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch)
     (required): a keyword identifying the CPU architecture that the returned package should be compatible with, like "amd64" or "arm".

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-request-1)
Sample Request

    curl 'https://registry.terraform.io/v1/providers/hashicorp/random/2.0.0/download/linux/amd64'
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#sample-response-1)
Sample Response

    {
      "protocols": ["4.0", "5.1"],
      "os": "linux",
      "arch": "amd64",
      "filename": "terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "download_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_linux_amd64.zip",
      "shasums_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS",
      "shasums_signature_url": "https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform-provider-random/2.0.0/terraform-provider-random_2.0.0_SHA256SUMS.sig",
      "shasum": "5f9c7aa76b7c34d722fc9123208e26b22d60440cb47150dd04733b9b94f4541a",
      "signing_keys": {
        "gpg_public_keys": [\
          {\
            "key_id": "51852D87348FFC4C",\
            "ascii_armor": "-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----\nVersion: GnuPG v1\n\nmQENBFMORM0BCADBRyKO1MhCirazOSVwcfTr1xUxjPvfxD3hjUwHtjsOy/bT6p9f\nW2mRPfwnq2JB5As+paL3UGDsSRDnK9KAxQb0NNF4+eVhr/EJ18s3wwXXDMjpIifq\nfIm2WyH3G+aRLTLPIpscUNKDyxFOUbsmgXAmJ46Re1fn8uKxKRHbfa39aeuEYWFA\n3drdL1WoUngvED7f+RnKBK2G6ZEpO+LDovQk19xGjiMTtPJrjMjZJ3QXqPvx5wca\nKSZLr4lMTuoTI/ZXyZy5bD4tShiZz6KcyX27cD70q2iRcEZ0poLKHyEIDAi3TM5k\nSwbbWBFd5RNPOR0qzrb/0p9ksKK48IIfH2FvABEBAAG0K0hhc2hpQ29ycCBTZWN1\ncml0eSA8c2VjdXJpdHlAaGFzaGljb3JwLmNvbT6JATgEEwECACIFAlMORM0CGwMG\nCwkIBwMCBhUIAgkKCwQWAgMBAh4BAheAAAoJEFGFLYc0j/xMyWIIAIPhcVqiQ59n\nJc07gjUX0SWBJAxEG1lKxfzS4Xp+57h2xxTpdotGQ1fZwsihaIqow337YHQI3q0i\nSqV534Ms+j/tU7X8sq11xFJIeEVG8PASRCwmryUwghFKPlHETQ8jJ+Y8+1asRydi\npsP3B/5Mjhqv/uOK+Vy3zAyIpyDOMtIpOVfjSpCplVRdtSTFWBu9Em7j5I2HMn1w\nsJZnJgXKpybpibGiiTtmnFLOwibmprSu04rsnP4ncdC2XRD4wIjoyA+4PKgX3sCO\nklEzKryWYBmLkJOMDdo52LttP3279s7XrkLEE7ia0fXa2c12EQ0f0DQ1tGUvyVEW\nWmJVccm5bq25AQ0EUw5EzQEIANaPUY04/g7AmYkOMjaCZ6iTp9hB5Rsj/4ee/ln9\nwArzRO9+3eejLWh53FoN1rO+su7tiXJA5YAzVy6tuolrqjM8DBztPxdLBbEi4V+j\n2tK0dATdBQBHEh3OJApO2UBtcjaZBT31zrG9K55D+CrcgIVEHAKY8Cb4kLBkb5wM\nskn+DrASKU0BNIV1qRsxfiUdQHZfSqtp004nrql1lbFMLFEuiY8FZrkkQ9qduixo\nmTT6f34/oiY+Jam3zCK7RDN/OjuWheIPGj/Qbx9JuNiwgX6yRj7OE1tjUx6d8g9y\n0H1fmLJbb3WZZbuuGFnK6qrE3bGeY8+AWaJAZ37wpWh1p0cAEQEAAYkBHwQYAQIA\nCQUCUw5EzQIbDAAKCRBRhS2HNI/8TJntCAClU7TOO/X053eKF1jqNW4A1qpxctVc\nz8eTcY8Om5O4f6a/rfxfNFKn9Qyja/OG1xWNobETy7MiMXYjaa8uUx5iFy6kMVaP\n0BXJ59NLZjMARGw6lVTYDTIvzqqqwLxgliSDfSnqUhubGwvykANPO+93BBx89MRG\nunNoYGXtPlhNFrAsB1VR8+EyKLv2HQtGCPSFBhrjuzH3gxGibNDDdFQLxxuJWepJ\nEK1UbTS4ms0NgZ2Uknqn1WRU1Ki7rE4sTy68iZtWpKQXZEJa0IGnuI2sSINGcXCJ\noEIgXTMyCILo34Fa/C6VCm2WBgz9zZO8/rHIiQm1J5zqz0DrDwKBUM9C\n=LYpS\n-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----",\
            "trust_signature": "",\
            "source": "HashiCorp",\
            "source_url": "https://www.hashicorp.com/security.html"\
          }\
        ]
      }
    }
    

### [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#response-properties-1)
Response Properties

A successful result is a JSON object with the following properties:

*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`protocols`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#protocols-1)
     (required): an array of Terraform provider API versions that the provider supports, in the same format as for [List Available Versions](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#list-available-versions)
    .
    
    While this property is optional when listing available options, it is _required_ for describing an individual provider package so that Terraform CLI can avoid downloading a package that will not be compatible with it.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`os`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#os-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `os` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`arch`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#arch-1)
     (required): this must echo back the `arch` parameter from the request.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`filename`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#filename)
     (required): the filename for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the "shasums" document, so that Terraform CLI can determine which of the given checksums should be used for this specific package.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`download_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#download_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve the provider's zip archive. If this is a relative URL then it will be resolved relative to the URL that returned the containing JSON object.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a text document recording expected SHA256 checksums for this package and possibly other packages for the same provider version on other platforms.
    
    The indicated document must be in the format generated by the `sha256` command available on many Unix systems, with one entry recording the same filename given in the `filename` property (case sensitive).
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasums_signature_url`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasums_signature_url)
     (required): a URL from which Terraform can retrieve a binary, detached GPG signature for the document at `shasums_url`, signed by one of the keys indicated in the `signing_keys` property.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`shasum`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#shasum)
     (required): the SHA256 checksum for this provider's zip archive as recorded in the shasums document.
    
*   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
    
    [`signing_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#signing_keys)
     (required): an object describing signing keys for this provider package, one of which must have been used to produce the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. The object has the following nested properties:
    
    *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
        
        [`gpg_public_keys`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#gpg_public_keys)
         (required): an array of objects, each describing one GPG signing key that is allowed to sign the checksums for this provider version. At least one element must be included, representing the key that produced the signature at `shasums_signature_url`. These objects have the following nested properties:
        
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`key_id`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#key_id)
             (required): uppercase-hexadecimal-formatted ID for this GPG key
            
        *   [](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#)
            
            [`ascii_armor`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/v1.7.x/provider-registry-protocol#ascii_armor)
             (required): an "ascii-armor" encoding of the **public key** associated with this GPG key.
